added: switch_plugin.py for activating vault versions without rebuilding
changed: /capabilities now advertises avatar-property mutation and direct avatar export support
/save-project now queues work onto the main-thread command queue
Build flow moves to env-driven shared build entry point
Windows: g_serverRunning and g_queueProcessing converted to std::atomic<bool>
Windows: /status reads g_patternsLoaded atomic instead of calling CLO API from HTTP thread
Windows: listen address changed from 0.0.0.0 to 127.0.0.1
Windows: queue drain timer interval reduced from 500ms to 200ms
Windows: PluginShutdown() wired to DLL_PROCESS_DETACH for clean unload
Mac: CLO_EXPORT and DYLIB_DESTRUCTOR macros guarded for Windows IntelliSense compatibility
fixed: Direct save-project execution from the HTTP thread
resolved: Machine-specific build values are no longer intended to live in active hardcoded scripts
known_issues: Native avatar measurement CSV import remains under investigation
Windows read endpoints (GET /patterns/*, /arrangement-list, etc.) call CLO API from HTTP thread — Mac uses safer dispatchSyncRead pattern
Windows /avatars/state: CLO API calls crash server thread via unhandled SEH exception — stubbed out, returns error
Windows export-avatar-avt: ExportAVT crashes CLO main thread via SEH exception — stubbed out, has_avatar_avt_export advertised as false; use zprj extraction

notes: Use the latest allowed plugin only. Windows and Mac should match endpoint behavior before both are marked in_sync.
